---
title: "Inspecting_dats"
author: "Sophie.Alice"
date: "2026-07-15"
output: workflowr::wflow_html
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

# Setup

```{r, message=FALSE, warning = FALSE}
rm(list = ls())
gc()

library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)
library(OlinkAnalyze)
library(DT)
library(limma)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(ggrepel)
```


# Functions

```{r, warning = FALSE}


##################################### Tables

library(DT)
show_table <- function(df) {
  DT::datatable(
    df,
    extensions = 'Buttons', 
    options = list(
      dom = 'Blfrtip',                       
      buttons = c('copy', 'csv', 'excel', 'pdf'),
      pageLength = 10,                      
      lengthMenu = c(10, 25, 50, 100),
      autoWidth = TRUE,
      columnDefs = list(list(
        targets = '_all',
        className = 'dt-nowrap'
      ))
    ),
    filter = 'top',                         
    class = 'cell-border stripe hover nowrap'       
  )
}


#################################  PCA Plots

plot_pca_dataset <- function(data, color_by = c("Group", "Experiment"), title_suffix = "") {
  color_by <- match.arg(color_by)
  
  # 1. Pivot data wide (Samples as rows, Assays as columns)
  npx_wide <- data |>
    dplyr::select(SampleID, OlinkID, NPX) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from = OlinkID, 
      values_from = NPX, 
      values_fn = mean
    ) |>
    tibble::column_to_rownames("SampleID")
  
  # 2. Drop assays with any NA across samples
  npx_wide_clean <- npx_wide[, colSums(is.na(npx_wide)) == 0]
  n_assays <- ncol(npx_wide_clean)
  
  # 3. Compute PCA
  pca_res <- prcomp(npx_wide_clean, scale. = TRUE)
  var_explained <- summary(pca_res)$importance[2, 1:2] * 100
  pc1_lab <- paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained[1], 1), "%)")
  pc2_lab <- paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained[2], 1), "%)")
  
  # 4. Extract metadata & join PCA coordinates
  meta_df <- data |>
    dplyr::select(SampleID, Group, Experiment) |>
    dplyr::distinct()
  
  pca_df <- as.data.frame(pca_res$x[, 1:2]) |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("SampleID") |>
    dplyr::left_join(meta_df, by = "SampleID")
  
  # 5. Setup plot configuration based on coloring factor
  if (color_by == "Group") {
    p_color_aes <- aes(color = Group)
    p_scale     <- scale_color_manual(values = custom_col5)
    color_title <- "Experimental Group"
  } else {
    p_color_aes <- aes(color = Experiment)
    p_scale     <- scale_color_manual(values = colors4)
    color_title <- "Experiment Batch"
  }
  
  # 6. Generate Plot
  ggplot2::ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(p_color_aes, size = 4, alpha = 0.85) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      aes(label = SampleID), 
      size = 3, 
      show.legend = FALSE, 
      max.overlaps = 15
    ) +
    p_scale +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste0("PCA: ", title_suffix),
      subtitle = paste0("Based on ", n_assays, " NPX protein profiles"),
      x = pc1_lab,
      y = pc2_lab,
      color = color_title
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
      plot.subtitle = ggplot2::element_text(face = "italic", size = 10),
      legend.position = "right",
      panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()
    )
}

```

# Set paths 

```{r, warning = FALSE}
data_path <- "/Users/sophiewagner/Desktop/USZ 25:26/Gabi_Project_Setup/Olink_Project/OLINK_Data/Result-X204SC26016367-Z01-F009-B1-43/1.Quant/pre_quant.csv"
fixed_lod_path <- "/Users/sophiewagner/Desktop/USZ 25:26/Gabi_Project_Setup/Olink_Project/OLINK_Data/Gino Links/Reveal Fixed LOD - csv file.csv"


# Visual Check: Display resolved paths in a table
paths_df <- tibble::tibble(
  File = c("Raw NPX File", "Fixed LOD File"),
  Path = c(data_path, fixed_lod_path),
  Exists = c(file.exists(data_path), file.exists(fixed_lod_path))
)

show_table(paths_df)


custom_palette <- colorRampPalette(c("#005EA8", "#6DB335", "#FFD966", "#EDAAD2", "#9F1C67"))

```

# Import raw NPX

```{r, warning = FALSE}
npx_raw <- OlinkAnalyze::read_npx(
  filename = data_path,
  out_df = "tibble",
  long_format = TRUE,
  olink_platform = "Reveal",
  data_type = "NPX",
  quiet = FALSE
)

show_table(head(npx_raw, 10))

```

# Check warnings

```{r, warning = FALSE}

#### Sample Quality

sample_counts <- npx_raw |>
  dplyr::distinct(SampleID, SampleType, SampleQC)
table(sample_counts$SampleQC)

#### Assay Quality

assay_counts <- npx_raw |>
  dplyr::distinct(OlinkID, Assay, AssayQC)

table(assay_counts$AssayQC)

### Check on the 1 protein that was issued a warning

warned_assay_info <- npx_raw |>
  dplyr::filter(AssayQC == "WARN") |>
  dplyr::distinct(OlinkID, Assay, Panel, AssayQC)

warned_assay_info

```

# Import LOD Reference

```{r, warning = FALSE}

fixed_lod <- readr::read_delim(
  file = fixed_lod_path,
  delim = ";",
  locale = readr::locale(decimal_mark = "."),
  trim_ws = TRUE,
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

# Only keep the proteins we used 

required_ref_ids <- sort(unique(npx_raw$DataAnalysisRefID))

fixed_lod_project <- fixed_lod |>
  dplyr::filter(
    DataAnalysisRefID %in% required_ref_ids,
    AssayType == "assay"
  )
lod_method_summary <- fixed_lod_project |>
  dplyr::count(DataAnalysisRefID, LODMethod, name = "Assay_Count")

lod_method_summary

#### check for dublicates

lod_key <- c("OlinkID", "DataAnalysisRefID")

duplicate_lod_defs <- fixed_lod_project |>
  dplyr::count(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(lod_key)), name = "n_defs") |>
  dplyr::filter(n_defs > 1)

duplicate_lod_defs # no duplicates found

#### Check if any proteins are missing 

missing_lod_defs <- npx_raw |>
  dplyr::filter(AssayType == "assay") |>
  dplyr::distinct(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(lod_key))) |>
  dplyr::anti_join(
    fixed_lod_project |> dplyr::distinct(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(lod_key))),
    by = lod_key
  )

missing_lod_defs # also 0
```

# Run check_npx

```{r, warning = FALSE}

npx_check <- OlinkAnalyze::check_npx(
  df = npx_raw,
  preferred_names = c("quant" = "NPX")
)

# check flagged assays
warned_assays <- npx_raw |>
  dplyr::filter(AssayQC == "WARN") |>
  dplyr::count(OlinkID, Assay, AssayQC, name = "Flagged_Samples")

warned_assays # still NPM1
```

# run clean_npx

```{r, warning = FALSE}

npx_pre_lod <- OlinkAnalyze::clean_npx(
  df = npx_raw,
  check_log = npx_check,
  remove_control_assay = FALSE,
  remove_control_sample = FALSE,
  remove_assay_warning = FALSE,
  remove_qc_warning = FALSE
)

# check if total dimensions remained unchanged 
dim_check <- tibble::tibble(
  Stage = c("Imported Raw Data", "Pre-LOD Cleaned Data"),
  Rows = c(nrow(npx_raw), nrow(npx_pre_lod)),
  Columns = c(ncol(npx_raw), ncol(npx_pre_lod))
)
dim_check # good
```

# integrate fixed LOD

```{r, warning = FALSE}
npx_pre_lod_check <- OlinkAnalyze::check_npx(
  df = npx_pre_lod,
  preferred_names = c("quant" = "NPX")
)

npx_with_lod <- OlinkAnalyze::olink_lod(
  data = npx_pre_lod,
  lod_method = "FixedLOD",
  lod_file_path = fixed_lod_path,
  check_log = npx_pre_lod_check
)

# Confirm addition of output columns (+2 new columns)
post_lod_dim <- tibble::tibble(
  Stage = c("Before olink_lod()", "After olink_lod()"),
  Rows = c(nrow(npx_pre_lod), nrow(npx_with_lod)),
  Columns = c(ncol(npx_pre_lod), ncol(npx_with_lod))
)

post_lod_dim # good
```

# Hybrid above-LOD classification (NPX and count)

```{r, warning = FALSE}

lod_lookup <- fixed_lod_project |>
  dplyr::select(
    dplyr::all_of(lod_key),
    LODMethod,
    FixedLODNPX_reference = LODNPX,
    FixedLODCount_reference = LODCount
  )

npx_qc_full <- npx_with_lod |>
  dplyr::left_join(lod_lookup, by = lod_key, relationship = "many-to-one") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    AboveLOD = dplyr::case_when(
      AssayType == "assay" & LODMethod == "lod_npx" & !is.na(NPX) & !is.na(LOD) ~ NPX > LOD,
      AssayType == "assay" & LODMethod == "lod_count" & !is.na(Count) & !is.na(FixedLODCount_reference) ~ Count > FixedLODCount_reference,
      TRUE ~ NA
    )
  )

# Visual Check: Classification summary breakdown
classification_summary <- npx_qc_full |>
  dplyr::filter(AssayType == "assay") |>
  dplyr::count(LODMethod, AboveLOD, name = "Observation_Count")

```



```{r,warning = FALSE, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}

###### Assay detection rates overall

detection_rates <- npx_qc_full |>
  dplyr::filter(AssayType == "assay", SampleType == "SAMPLE") |>
  dplyr::group_by(Assay, OlinkID, LODMethod) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    percent_above_lod = mean(AboveLOD, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

show_table(detection_rates)

# Visual Check: Histogram distribution of assay detection percentages
ggplot2::ggplot(detection_rates, aes(x = percent_above_lod, fill = LODMethod)) +
  ggplot2::geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, color = "black", alpha = 0.8, position = "stack") +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = c("lod_npx" = "#005EA8", "lod_count" = "#6DB335")) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Overall Protein Detection Rates Across Biological Samples",
    x = "Percent of Samples Above LOD (%)",
    y = "Number of Protein Assays",
    fill = "LOD Method"
  )

```

# percent over LOD per sample

```{r,warning = FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=10}

lod_summary <- npx_qc_full |>
  dplyr::filter(AssayType == "assay") |>
  dplyr::group_by(SampleID) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_above_LOD    = sum(AboveLOD == TRUE, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_total        = sum(!is.na(AboveLOD)),
    frac_above_LOD = n_above_LOD / n_total,
    Matrix         = if ("Matrix" %in% names(npx_qc_full)) dplyr::first(Matrix) else dplyr::first(SampleType),
    .groups        = "drop"
  )

colornum <- length(unique(lod_summary$Matrix))
selected_colors <- custom_palette(colornum)

ggplot2::ggplot(lod_summary,
       aes(x = reorder(SampleID, frac_above_LOD),
           y = frac_above_LOD,
           fill = Matrix)) +
  ggplot2::geom_col() +
  ggplot2::coord_flip() +
  ggplot2::geom_text(
    aes(
      label = n_above_LOD,
      y = frac_above_LOD + 0.03
    ),
    size = 2.8
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1.15), 
    labels = scales::percent_format()
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = selected_colors) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Protein Detection Rates per Sample",
    x     = "Sample",
    y     = "Fraction of proteins above LOD",
    fill  = "Matrix type"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 6),
    plot.title  = ggplot2::element_text( size = 11)
  )


```


# Distance to LOD per sample

```{r,warning = FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=10}

npx_lod_dist <- npx_qc_full |>
  dplyr::filter(AssayType == "assay") |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    lod_dist = NPX - LOD,
    Matrix   = if ("Matrix" %in% names(npx_qc_full)) Matrix else SampleType
  )

sample_order <- npx_lod_dist |>
  dplyr::group_by(SampleID) |>
  dplyr::summarise(median_lod_dist = median(lod_dist, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::arrange(median_lod_dist) |>
  dplyr::pull(SampleID)

npx_lod_dist$SampleID <- factor(npx_lod_dist$SampleID, levels = sample_order)

ggplot2::ggplot(npx_lod_dist,
       aes(x    = SampleID,
           y    = lod_dist,
           fill = Matrix)) +
  ggplot2::geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3, alpha = 0.85, outlier.alpha = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred", linewidth = 0.6) +
  ggplot2::coord_flip() +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = selected_colors) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Signal Distance to LOD (NPX - LOD) per Sample",
    x     = "Sample",
    y     = "Distance to LOD (NPX – LOD)",
    fill  = "Matrix type"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 6),
    plot.title  = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11)
  )

```


# Subset the samples of interes

```{r, warning = FALSE}

samples_to_keep <- c(
  # --- 1. MTs control ---
  "EX1_Con1_NC",   # Row 1:  EX1 Cont. 1 NC
  "EX1_Con2_NC",   # Row 2:  EX1 Cont. 2 NC  
  "EX2_Con1_NC",   # Row 3:  EX2 Cont. 1 NC
  "EX2_Con2_NC",   # Row 4:  EX2 Cont. 2 NC
  "EX3_Cont_NC",   # Row 5:  EX3 Cont. NC

  # --- 2. MTs TGFb ---
  "EX1_Con1_TGF",    # Row 6:  EX1 Cont. 1 TGF
  "EX1_Cont_2_TGF",  # Row 7:  EX1 Cont. 2 TGF
  "EX2_Con1_TGF",    # Row 8:  EX2 Cont. 1 TGF
  "EX2_Con2_TGF",    # Row 9:  EX2 Cont. 2 TGF
  "EX3_Cont_TGF",    # Row 10: EX3 Cont. TGF

  # --- 3. MTs control + healthy macrophages  ---
  "EX1_H_NC",   # Row 11: EX1 H NC
  "EX2_H_NC",   # Row 12: EX2 H NC
  "EX3_H1_NC",  # Row 13: EX3 H1 NC
  "EX3_H2_NC",  # Row 14: EX3 H2 NC
  "EX4_H1_NC",  # Row 15: EX4 H1 NC
  "EX4_H2_NC",  # Row 16: EX4 H2 NC

  # --- 4. MTs TGFb + healthy macrophages ---
  "EX1_H_TGF",   # Row 17: EX1 H TGF
  "EX2_H_TGF",   # Row 18: EX2 H TGF
  "EX3_H1_TGF",  # Row 19: EX3 H1 TGF
  "EX3_H2_TGF",  # Row 20: EX3 H2 TGF
  "EX4_H1_TGF",  # Row 21: EX4 H1 TGF
  "EX4_H2_TGF"  # Row 22: EX4 H2 TGF

  # --- 7. Media Controls ---
  #"HSM",        # Row 35: Heart standard medium
  #"MM_medium"   # Maintenance Medium
)

if (length(samples_to_keep) > 0) {
  npx_qc_subset <- npx_qc_full |>
    dplyr::filter(SampleType == "SAMPLE", SampleID %in% samples_to_keep)
} else {
  npx_qc_subset <- npx_qc_full |>
    dplyr::filter(SampleType == "SAMPLE")
}

# Visual Check: Verify subset size
subset_info <- tibble::tibble(
  Dataset = c("Full Plate (Biological)", "Filtered Cohort Subset"),
  Samples = c(
    dplyr::n_distinct(npx_qc_full$SampleID[npx_qc_full$SampleType == "SAMPLE"]),
    dplyr::n_distinct(npx_qc_subset$SampleID)
  ),
  Total_Observations = c(nrow(npx_qc_full[npx_qc_full$SampleType == "SAMPLE", ]), nrow(npx_qc_subset))
)

subset_info

```

# adding metadata

```{r, warning = FALSE}

npx_qc_pca_data <- npx_qc_subset |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    Experiment = dplyr::case_when(
      stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "^EX1") ~ "EX1",
      stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "^EX2") ~ "EX2",
      stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "^EX3") ~ "EX3",
      stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "^EX4") ~ "EX4",
      TRUE ~ "Media"
    ),
    Experiment = factor(Experiment),
    
    # Define experimental groups
    Group = dplyr::case_when(
      SampleID %in% c("HSM", "MM_medium") ~ "Media Control",
      stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "_NC$") & stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "_H") ~ "MT + Macrophages (NC)",
      stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "_TGF$") & stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "_H") ~ "MT + Macrophages (TGFb)",
      stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "_NC$") ~ "MT Control (NC)",
      stringr::str_detect(SampleID, "_TGF$") ~ "MT Control (TGFb)",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    Group = factor(Group, levels = c(
      "MT Control (NC)", 
      "MT Control (TGFb)", 
      "MT + Macrophages (NC)", 
      "MT + Macrophages (TGFb)", 
      "Media Control"
    ))
  )

```


# IQR - mNPX

```{r, warning = FALSE}

colors4 <- custom_palette(4)

# 1. Calculate Median and IQR per sample across all measured assays
sample_stats <- npx_qc_pca_data |>
  dplyr::group_by(SampleID, Experiment) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median_npx = median(NPX, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_npx = IQR(NPX, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_above_lod = mean(AboveLOD == TRUE, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 2. Plot Per-Sample Median vs. IQR colored by Experiment
ggplot2::ggplot(sample_stats, aes(x = median_npx, y = iqr_npx, color = Experiment)) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.9) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    aes(label = SampleID), 
    size = 3.2, 
    show.legend = FALSE, 
    max.overlaps = 15,
    box.padding = 0.3
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = colors4) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Batch Effect Check: Per-Sample Median NPX vs. IQR",
    subtitle = "Assess technical shifts and signal distributions across experiment batches",
    x = "Median NPX per Sample",
    y = "Interquartile Range (IQR) per Sample",
    color = "Experiment Batch"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()
  )


```


# Check detection rate

```{r, warning = FALSE}

# Standardize to biological samples only
bio_samples <- npx_qc_pca_data |>
  dplyr::filter(AssayType == "assay", SampleType == "SAMPLE")


# Per-Protein Detection Histogram ---
protein_detection <- bio_samples |>
  dplyr::group_by(OlinkID, Assay) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    pct_above_lod = mean(AboveLOD, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot2::ggplot(protein_detection, aes(x = pct_above_lod)) +
  ggplot2::geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "#005EA8", color = "white", boundary = 0) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = 25, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::annotate("text", x = 52, y = Inf, label = "25% Cutoff", vjust = 2, hjust = 0, color = "darkred", fontface = "bold") +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 105), breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Distribution of Protein Detection Rates Across Samples",
    subtitle = "Percentage of biological samples with signal > LOD per protein assay",
    x = "Detection Rate per Protein (% above LOD)",
    y = "Number of Protein Assays"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()
  )


# Per-Sample Detection Histogram ---

sample_detection <- bio_samples |>
  dplyr::group_by(SampleID, Group) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    pct_above_lod = mean(AboveLOD, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot2::ggplot(sample_detection, aes(x = pct_above_lod)) +
  ggplot2::geom_histogram(binwidth = 2, fill = "#6DB335", color = "white", boundary = 0) +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 105), breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Distribution of Sample Detection Rates Across Assays",
    subtitle = "Percentage of protein assays detected per individual sample",
    x = "Detection Rate per Sample (% proteins above LOD)",
    y = "Number of Samples"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = ggplot2::element_blank()
  )

########################################################

npx_lod_dist <- bio_samples |>
  dplyr::mutate(lod_dist = NPX - LOD)

# Set sample order
sample_order <- sort(unique(npx_lod_dist$SampleID))
npx_lod_dist$SampleID <- factor(npx_lod_dist$SampleID, levels = sample_order)

# Generate Boxplot
ggplot2::ggplot(npx_lod_dist,
       aes(x    = SampleID,
           y    = lod_dist,
           fill = Experiment)) +
  ggplot2::geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3, alpha = 0.85, outlier.alpha = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred", linewidth = 0.6) +
  ggplot2::coord_flip() +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = colors4) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Signal Distance to LOD (NPX - LOD) per Sample",
    subtitle = "Dashed line at 0 marks the Limit of Detection (LOD)",
    x     = "Sample",
    y     = "Distance to LOD (NPX – LOD)",
    fill  = "Experiment Batch"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw() +
  ggplot2::theme(
    axis.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 7),
    plot.title  = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11, face = "bold")
  )

##############################################


# 1. Ensure lod_dist is calculated
bio_samples <- bio_samples |>
  dplyr::mutate(lod_dist = NPX - LOD)

# 2. Plot Distance to LOD grouped by Experimental Group
ggplot2::ggplot(bio_samples, aes(x = Group, y = lod_dist, fill = Group)) +
  ggplot2::geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3, alpha = 0.85, outlier.alpha = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "darkred", linewidth = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::coord_flip() +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = colors4) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Signal Distance to LOD (NPX - LOD) per Condition",
    subtitle = "Dashed line at 0 marks the Limit of Detection (LOD)",
    x = "Experimental Condition",
    y = "Distance to LOD (NPX - LOD)",
    fill = "Condition"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )


######################## hybrid filtering 

# per-protein metrics on biological samples
protein_metrics <- bio_samples |>
  dplyr::group_by(OlinkID, Assay) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_above_lod = sum(AboveLOD == TRUE, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_val     = IQR(NPX, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )

# Define IQR threshold 
iqr_threshold <- median(protein_metrics$iqr_val, na.rm = TRUE)

# Identify proteins passing AT LEAST ONE rule
keep_hybrid_proteins <- protein_metrics |>
  dplyr::filter(n_above_lod >= 2 | iqr_val >= iqr_threshold) |>
  dplyr::pull(OlinkID) |>
  unique()

# Filter the primary biological dataset
npx_filtered <- bio_samples |>
  dplyr::filter(OlinkID %in% keep_hybrid_proteins)

# Diagnostic Summary
total_assays    <- dplyr::n_distinct(bio_samples$OlinkID)
retained_hybrid <- length(keep_hybrid_proteins)

cat(sprintf(
  "=== Hybrid Protein Detection Filtering Summary ===\nTotal assays evaluated: %d\nRetained assays (n_above_lod >= 2 OR IQR >= %.3f): %d (%.1f%%)\nDropped assays: %d (%.1f%%)\n",
  total_assays,
  iqr_threshold,
  retained_hybrid, (retained_hybrid / total_assays) * 100,
  total_assays - retained_hybrid, ((total_assays - retained_hybrid) / total_assays) * 100
))

```  

# generate PCAs

```{r, warning = FALSE}

custom_col5 <- custom_palette(5)
colors4     <- custom_palette(4)


# Unfiltered - Colored by Group
p_unfiltered_group <- plot_pca_dataset(
  data = npx_qc_pca_data, 
  color_by = "Group", 
  title_suffix = "Unfiltered Dataset (Colored by Condition)"
)

# Unfiltered - Colored by Experiment Batch
p_unfiltered_exp <- plot_pca_dataset(
  data = npx_qc_pca_data, 
  color_by = "Experiment", 
  title_suffix = "Unfiltered Dataset (Colored by Batch)"
)

# Filtered (Hybrid) - Colored by Group
p_filtered_group <- plot_pca_dataset(
  data = npx_filtered, 
  color_by = "Group", 
  title_suffix = "Hybrid Filtered Dataset (Colored by Condition)"
)

# Filtered (Hybrid) - Colored by Experiment Batch
p_filtered_exp <- plot_pca_dataset(
  data = npx_filtered, 
  color_by = "Experiment", 
  title_suffix = "Hybrid Filtered Dataset (Colored by Batch)"
)

# Render plots
p_unfiltered_group
p_unfiltered_exp

# Render plots
p_filtered_group
p_filtered_exp

```

# DPEA with limma

```{r, warning = FALSE}

# clean_meta <- npx_qc_pca_data |> 
#   dplyr::select(SampleID, Group, Experiment) |> 
#   dplyr::distinct() |> 
#   dplyr::filter(Group != "Other", Group != "Media Control") 
# 
# clean_meta$Group <- factor(clean_meta$Group, levels = c(
#   "MT Control (NC)", 
#   "MT Control (TGFb)", 
#   "MT + Macrophages (NC)", 
#   "MT + Macrophages (TGFb)"
# ))
# 
# design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + Group + Experiment, data = clean_meta)
# colnames(design) <- make.names(colnames(design))
# 
# # Prepare NPX Expression Matrix ---
# npx_matrix <- npx_qc_pca_data |>
#   dplyr::filter(SampleID %in% clean_meta$SampleID) |>
#   dplyr::select(SampleID, Assay, NPX) |>
#   tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = SampleID, values_from = NPX) |>
#   column_to_rownames("Assay") |>
#   as.matrix()
# 
# npx_matrix <- npx_matrix[, clean_meta$SampleID]
# 
# # Fit Linear Model ---
# fit <- lmFit(npx_matrix, design)
# 
# # Define Specific Contrasts ---
# contrast_matrix <- makeContrasts(
#   # 1. CMF_TGF vs CMF_NC (Effect of TGFb in MTs alone)
#   Contrast1_CMFTGF_vs_CMFNC = GroupMT.Control..TGFb. - GroupMT.Control..NC.,
#   # 2. CMFHM_NC vs CMF_NC (Effect of Healthy Macrophages baseline)
#   Contrast2_CMFHMNC_vs_CMFNC = GroupMT...Macrophages..NC. - GroupMT.Control..NC.,
#   # 3. CMFHM_TGF vs CMFHM_NC (Effect of TGFb in co-culture)
#   Contrast3_CMFHMTGF_vs_CMFHMNC = GroupMT...Macrophages..TGFb. - GroupMT...Macrophages..NC.,
#   # 4. CMFHM_TGF vs CMF_TGF (Effect of Healthy Macrophages under TGFb)
#   Contrast4_CMFHMTGF_vs_CMFTGF = GroupMT...Macrophages..TGFb. - GroupMT.Control..TGFb.,
#   levels = design
# )
# 
# # Fit Contrasts & Empirical Bayes Moderation ---
# fit_eb <- eBayes(contrasts.fit(fit, contrast_matrix))
# 
# # Extract Results Tables for All 4 Contrasts ---
# res_contrast1 <- topTable(fit_eb, coef = "Contrast1_CMFTGF_vs_CMFNC", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH") |> rownames_to_column("Assay")
# res_contrast2 <- topTable(fit_eb, coef = "Contrast2_CMFHMNC_vs_CMFNC", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH") |> rownames_to_column("Assay")
# res_contrast3 <- topTable(fit_eb, coef = "Contrast3_CMFHMTGF_vs_CMFHMNC", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH") |> rownames_to_column("Assay")
# res_contrast4 <- topTable(fit_eb, coef = "Contrast4_CMFHMTGF_vs_CMFTGF", number = Inf, adjust.method = "BH") |> rownames_to_column("Assay")
```

```{r, warning = FALSE}


```

```{r, warning = FALSE}

```

```{r, warning = FALSE}

```

```{r, warning = FALSE}

```

```{r, warning = FALSE}


```

```{r, warning = FALSE}

```

```{r, warning = FALSE}

```

```{r, warning = FALSE}

```

```{r, warning = FALSE}


```

```{r, warning = FALSE}

```

```{r, warning = FALSE}

```

```{r, warning = FALSE}

```
